description: This API routine is obsolete. It has been replaced by the API routine dist_scmu_fault_2, and is implemented by a call to dist_scmu_fault_2. Use this API to calculate an unbalanced fault and apply the equivalent positive sequence fault admittance at a bus during dynamic simulations. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.2.0. ierr = dist_scmu_fault(options, values)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: An array of four elements specifying options and values. The first two elements of OPTIONS define options to be used in the construction of the sequence admittance matrices; the remaining entries define the fault to be applied. (input).

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: An array of four elements specifying values used to define the fault. They are dependent on the value specified for OPTIONS(3). (input).

options.description: dc line and FACTS device option (0 by default).
transformer impedance correction option (1 by default).
do not apply to zero sequence.
bus to be faulted (no default allowed).

values.description: resistance of the complex L-G (if OPTIONS(3) is 1) or L-L (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) fault impedance in pu (0.0 by default).
reactance of the complex L-G (if OPTIONS(3) is 1) or L-L (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) fault impedance in pu (0.0 by default).
resistance of the complex L-G fault impedance in pu (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) (0.0 by default).
reactance of the complex L-G fault impedance in pu (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) (0.0 by default).

error_codes.description: STRT or MSTR not successfully executed.
fixed bus shunt table is full.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
